transcript:
Front desk: Confirming your details — phone 415-555-1004, email patient4@example.com, date of birth 05/15/1984, SSN 123-45-1004, MRN: SYN1004.
Doctor: Good morning! What brings you in today?
Patient: Hi, I think I sprained my ankle.
Doctor: I see. Can you tell me how it happened?
Patient: I was playing basketball last night and landed awkwardly on it.
Doctor: Got it. On a scale from 1 to 10, how would you rate your pain?
Patient: It's about a 7 right now.
Doctor: Alright. Let's take a look. I’ll check your vitals first. Blood pressure is 120 over 80, heart rate is 72, and temperature is 98.6 degrees Fahrenheit. 
Patient: Okay.
Doctor: Now, can you move your ankle at all?
Patient: Not really, it hurts too much.
Doctor: I can see some swelling. Have you taken anything for the pain?
Patient: I took some ibuprofen earlier.
Doctor: How much did you take?
Patient: I took 400 milligrams.
Doctor: Good choice. Do you have any allergies to medications?
Patient: No, I don’t have any allergies.
Doctor: Perfect. Based on what you’ve described, it sounds like a moderate sprain. I recommend rest, ice, compression, and elevation. You can continue taking ibuprofen for the pain. I will also give you a brace to support your ankle. 
Patient: Sounds good. How long will it take to heal?
Doctor: Usually, it takes about 2 to 6 weeks, depending on the severity of the sprain. We’ll schedule a follow-up in a week to check on your progress.
Patient: Thank you, Doctor!
